OCR Benchmark Analysis Report - SROIE 2019 Dataset

This report provides a comprehensive evaluation of multiple OCR engines (EasyOCR, Tesseract, PaddleOCR) executed on the SROIE 2019 dataset.

Executive Summary & Key Insights

  • Best Overall Accuracy: EasyOCR achieved the highest Character Accuracy at 78.22% and Word Accuracy at 44.45%.
  • Best F1-Score: EasyOCR produced the highest F1-score (0.310), indicating the best balance between precision and recall.
  • Failed Models: Models PaddleOCR, Tesseract achieved exceptionally low scores (<5%), suggesting missing underlying dependencies (e.g., Tesseract binaries) or models failing to initialize properly in this environment.

2. Results and Discussion

The charts below present the performance of the tested OCR engines across multiple dimensions. Accuracy Metrics (Character and Word Accuracy) measure exact match rates, while Error Rates (CER and WER) quantify the edit distance needed to correct the predicted text. Precision, Recall, and F1-score are calculated using a Bag-of-Words approach to measure the engines' ability to retrieve the correct vocabulary regardless of ordering.
